title: Trace Font-Relative Reference Contexts
summary: Build and verify css font relative units in survey typography and component scaling audit through prediction, inspection, explanation, cumulative code, and changed-case transfer.
objectives: Explain css font relative units from purpose, behavior, and evidence.
Apply css font relative units without discarding earlier valid work.
Diagnose the named misconception in changed content, input, and failure states.

steps.title: 05 · Read the survey typography and component scaling audit case · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 5: Which claim preserves css font relative units when data, input, and layout change? Scenario: survey typography and component scaling audit.
steps.why: Trace Font-Relative Reference Contexts makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 5 in survey typography and component scaling audit.

content.text: Rem resolves from the root font size; em usually resolves from the element font size and can compound when used for font-size; ch approximates the width of the zero glyph; lh follows line height; rlh follows root line height. Font-relative units can respect user preferences, but only when their reference context and compounding behavior are understood. Apply it to survey typography and component scaling audit.

Survey typography and component scaling audit case 5: use em at every nesting level because compounding always preserves the same visual size.
